chunk_summary: This section explores case vignettes illustrating follow-up algorithms and referral decisions for managing moderate acute malnutrition (MAM) in children, emphasizing escalation pathways and community-based interventions.
learning_objectives: Understand the follow-up algorithms for managing moderate acute malnutrition (MAM) in children under 5 years.
Identify appropriate referral decisions and escalation pathways for MAM cases based on clinical scenarios.
Analyze case vignettes to apply community-based management strategies for MAM.
Review guidelines for supplementary feeding programs and their integration into MAM management.
key_facts: Moderate acute malnutrition (MAM) affects children under 5 years and requires structured follow-up and referral pathways.
Community-based management of acute malnutrition (CMAM) is a key strategy endorsed by NITI Aayog and UNICEF in India.
Supplementary Nutrition (ICDS) Rules, 2017, provide guidelines for feeding programs targeting malnourished children.
Paediatric TB management guidelines highlight the intersection of malnutrition and TB in vulnerable populations.
Escalation pathways include referral to specialized care for severe cases or complications.
reference_quote: Community-based management of acute malnutrition (CMAM) is a key strategy endorsed by NITI Aayog and UNICEF to address MAM in India. (Source: https://www.niti.gov.in/sites/default/files/2023-12/CMAM-report-NITI-Aayog.pdf)
